text:
Once upon a sunny afternoon, little mouse Harold found a golden necklace at a flea market. He loved it. Every evening he'd wear it around his neck while he sketched tiny scenes on tiny papers.

One night, as he admired his own reflection in a glass jar, he noticed a peculiar sparkle. Could it be...?

With excitement, Harold tucked the necklace into his pocketbook. The next day at the park, his reflection was dancing with something gleaming. He dived into the merry-go-round only to pull out a tiny, golden key.

Curious, he pocketed it, skipping home.

That night, he dreamst of a castle guarded by tiny guardian mice who whispered secrets of treasure troves.

Waking up, Harold hurried to his cabinet, and there it sat—the key. With a bounce in his hopper, he retraced his steps.

First he visited Mr. Fluffington, the wise old owl, who hummed about old mines. After some careful
